utterance: show me comedies from the 80s
note: genre+decade filter must surface Comedy-tagged 1980s titles

utterance: show me horror movies
note: pure Horror genre filter; genre tag and genome score must align

utterance: I liked Inception and Interstellar, recommend me something similar
note: Mode-1 Nolan sci-fi anchors must surface Sci-Fi neighbors

utterance: I want something funny to watch tonight, nothing scary please
note: Comedy require + Horror hard exclude must both hold in top-10

utterance: romantic comedies please
note: AND-genre filter must surface rom-coms; neither genre should dilute

utterance: I loved The Godfather and Goodfellas, what should I watch next?
note: Mode-1 crime-mafia anchors must pull Crime neighbors without Comedy bleed

utterance: [HOLE] I only have 90 minutes tonight, show me nothing longer than that
note: runtime cap unbuilt; no runtime data in feature store or serving

utterance: French heist films from the 70s
note: year+country stack thins pool; heist genome must persist after country-fallback | step C: require_country=FR + year + Crime compose; the 70s French-heist set is genuinely thin, so both the Crime and heist-genome proxies are min>=1, not corpus-unrealistic >=4. ORIGIN-COUNTRY recalibration: with require_country reading origin_country (nationality) not production_countries, the only genuinely French-origin 70s Crime film in the corpus is Le Cercle Rouge (Melville, 1970); the 4 that dropped (Marathon Man US, The American Friend DE, The Night Porter IT, Day of the Jackal US/GB) were co-financed non-French films that used to leak in — the same co-production noise the origin switch removes.

utterance: a feel-good movie about cooking
note: food genome must anchor; absent mood slot risks grim food films or comfort-genre flood

utterance: epic space opera but more mature, not the kiddie stuff
note: space opera genome and Animation exclusion must jointly hold; genre-only fallback loses space opera signal

utterance: horror comedies please, nothing too gory
note: dual-genre thin pool: collapse to Comedy-only or Horror-only is the primary failure

utterance: romantic movie set in Paris, more French New Wave than Hollywood
note: without require_country Hollywood romances flood results; paris genome alone insufficient | step C: require_country=FR filters to French productions; membership is the primary metric.

utterance: slick action heist with a big ensemble cast like Ocean's Eleven
note: anchor must not dilute heist genome toward generic blockbusters; heist must dominate top-10

utterance: [HOLE] superhero film but nothing from the MCU or DC Extended Universe
note: franchise exclusion unrepresentable by genre/genome/people; no engine covers MCU or DC membership

utterance: Recommend me psychological thrillers but absolutely no horror — nothing scary or gory
note: LLM must not conflate Thriller with Horror in exclude_genres

utterance: I want a serious love story — romance is great but I cannot stand romantic comedies, nothing silly or lighthearted
note: cannot stand romcom must set Comedy exclusion not Romance exclusion

utterance: Classic film noir crime movies from before 1960, but not Double Indemnity or The Maltese Falcon — I've already seen those
note: but not X or Y clause must parse to exclude_titles list not be dropped

utterance: Sci-fi films please, but I have had enough of the Alien franchise — nothing from that series at all
note: franchise exclusion must block Alien/Aliens/Alien3; Prometheus gap unfixable by title-substr

utterance: Something uplifting and feel-good — absolutely nothing sad or depressing, I need cheering up after a rough week
note: nothing sad must populate exclude_mood not be silently dropped by extractor

utterance: Action and adventure but no animated films at all — live-action only please, nothing cartoon
note: live-action only paraphrase must map to exclude Animation without being ignored

utterance: [HOLE] I want atmospheric suspenseful films but nothing gory at all — I genuinely cannot handle graphic violence or blood
note: no exclude_genome_tags slot in schema; no excludes_genome assertion type exists in runner

utterance: I want movies actually set in Paris, not just French cinema in general
note: paris genome hard-floor gates the pool to Paris-set films; min_genome (>=6 at >=0.5) verifies it. Rome-set La Dolce Vita (paris ~0.08) is dropped by the floor.

utterance: Give me gripping courtroom dramas - trials where the stakes are life or death
note: courtroom tag blocks crime films where no trial scenes appear

utterance: I love time travel films where the rules are airtight and internally consistent
note: time travel genome gate blocks sci-fi action films with no time mechanism

utterance: I only want biopics or films based on real events - nothing fictional even if it feels historical
note: true-story gate prevents fictional historical dramas from diluting biopics

utterance: Movies set during World War II itself - not the Cold War or postwar fallout
note: world war ii genome hard-floor gates the pool to WWII films; min_genome (>=6 at >=0.5) verifies it. Vietnam-set Full Metal Jacket (wwii ~0.13) and Cold-War films are dropped.

utterance: Dark serious dystopian futures where society has collapsed or turned authoritarian - not satirical comedies
note: dystopia gate with Comedy cap separates grim futures from satirical ones

utterance: [HOLE] Movies where everything happens in a single night - I want that one-night pressure and real-time intensity
note: no genome tag for single-night story span; duration filter absent from all three engines

utterance: I want movies like Pulp Fiction
note: era-1994 drift: Jurassic Park / Forrest Gump must not appear

utterance: Show me films like Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
note: wuxia anchor must not drift to 2000-2001 era blockbusters

utterance: something like Mulholland Drive
note: Lynch surrealist anchor must not drift to 2001-2002 Hollywood blockbusters

utterance: give me films like Oldboy
note: 2003 Korean anchor must not drift to Pirates / RotK era neighbors

utterance: I loved Amélie, show me similar movies
note: French whimsy anchor must not drift to 2001 animated blockbusters

utterance: more movies like The Seventh Seal
note: 1957 Bergman anchor must not drift to 1950s Hollywood spectacles

utterance: [HOLE] movies like Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon but only originally in Chinese

assertions.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon (Wo hu cang long) (2000)

note: no wired language filter; era drift persists for foreign-language anchors | step C: require_original_language=zh gates to Chinese-language films (drops the en HP/LotR co-watch neighbours directly).

utterance: I'm in the mood for a romance, something that makes you feel good about love
note: romance vibe must not drift to Bergman arthouse drama via mood anchors

utterance: I want to watch Christmas movies and really get into the holiday spirit
note: holiday request must surface Christmas-genome films, not generic drama

utterance: I'd love some fun fantasy movies, the kind with magic and adventure and a real sense of wonder
note: fun fantasy must not become dark arthouse-horror via mood synthesis

utterance: I'm a huge fan of westerns, give me some great ones
note: pure western taste must yield Western-genre films, not Crime-drama drift

utterance: Give me gritty crime thrillers, I love the tension and suspense
note: crime-thriller vibe must not collapse to prestige arthouse drama

utterance: War movies, I want the intense battlefield kind
note: war vibe must surface War-genre films, not general intense Drama

utterance: [HOLE] I want something light and fun, absolutely nothing dark or depressing
note: exclude_mood anti-floor drops the horror-comedy (Shaun of the Dead, dark 0.46) so 'nothing dark' clears Horror; liked_genres Comedy + humor/lighthearted keep Drama low. built=true: guards the shipped anti-floor.

utterance: I want something that will make me cry
note: raw mood phrase must route to heartbreaking genome tag, not literal Comedy

utterance: I need something cozy and warm for a Sunday afternoon
note: cozy/warm must route to feel-good genome, not Comedy genre literal

utterance: I usually watch comedies but tonight I want something darker and more mature
note: mood pivot must surface dark genome and suppress Comedy anti-dilution

utterance: Give me something psychologically dark and unsettling, but nothing with jump scares or gore
note: dark mood must activate dark genome AND hard-exclude Horror simultaneously

utterance: Something feel-good but not mindless — a drama that leaves me smiling
note: feel-good genome anchors and Drama genre must co-occur in top results

utterance: I loved The Dark Knight but I'm in the mood for something more emotional and heartbreaking tonight
note: mood genome anchors must dominate liked-item style; Action must not flood

utterance: [HOLE] I'm already stressed and sad — I want nothing heavy or heartbreaking, just something light and fun
note: exclude_mood anti-floor removes heavy/heartbreaking films. Faithful test is the AFFECT (heartbreaking absent), NOT the Drama genre LABEL: the feel-good anchor surfaces feel-good dramedies (Marigold Hotel, Chocolat) that are light but Drama-tagged, so the old max_genre Drama<=3 was unachievable by affect exclusion. min_genome feel-good confirms the positive intent still holds; max_genome heartbreaking@0.3 (below the 0.4 anti-floor ceiling) confirms the suppression reaches beyond the hard cut.

utterance: Show me Tom Hanks movies
note: baseline require_people: all top-k must include Tom Hanks

utterance: I love The Prestige and Memento but want smart mind-bending thrillers beyond Nolan
note: exclude_people blocks director even when liked seeds are his own films

utterance: Movies directed by Sofia Coppola
note: directed-by phrasing must route to require_people, not liked_items

utterance: What films did Tom Hanks and Steven Spielberg make together?
note: multi-person require is intersection: every result needs both people

utterance: Denzel Washington action movies
note: person + genre hard filters apply simultaneously, not as separate fallbacks

utterance: I love Hans Zimmer's music — show me movies he scored
note: composer role is absent from actor/director/writer facets; filter always misses

utterance: [HOLE] Show me A24 horror films
note: studio membership is a people-adjacent entity none of the three engines cover

utterance: I want something with frontier outlaw vibes but set in space, like a space western - guns and adventure on spaceships
note: No anchor; rare Sci-Fi+Western AND-pool must not collapse to pure Sci-Fi

utterance: I love horror like The Shining and comedies like Airplane! but I want both at the same time - a horror comedy
note: Shining (Horror-only) and Airplane (Comedy-only) both filtered; zero anchor signal remains | step C (intersection-first, 2026-07-02): utterance demands the intersection ('both in the same film'); strict AND returns 10/10 genuine both-genre films — assert intersection, not the mix the old max_genre cap encoded.

utterance: A sci-fi noir please - futuristic world, dark detective atmosphere, like Blade Runner's visual style and mood
note: Blade Runner carries no Film-Noir label; genome neo-noir must rescue the signal

utterance: I want a romantic thriller - real danger and stakes like Gone Girl, but also the emotional love story of The Notebook, both together
note: Gone Girl (Thriller-only) + Notebook (Romance-only) both filtered; Drama must not swamp both | step C (intersection-first, 2026-07-02): utterance demands the intersection ('both in the same film'); strict AND returns 10/10 genuine both-genre films — assert intersection, not the mix the old max_genre cap encoded.

utterance: Dark fantasy horror - a magical world but genuinely terrifying, like Pan's Labyrinth but with more actual horror
note: Pan's Labyrinth tagged Fantasy/Drama not Horror; filtered anchor leaves Horror swamp risk | step C (intersection-first, 2026-07-02): utterance demands the intersection ('both in the same film'); strict AND returns 10/10 genuine both-genre films — assert intersection, not the mix the old max_genre cap encoded.

utterance: Action comedy - real exciting action sequences like Die Hard but genuinely funny like Anchorman, both in the same film

liked_items: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy

note: Die Hard (Action-only) + Anchorman (Comedy-only) both filtered; high-volume Action swamps Comedy | step C (intersection-first, 2026-07-02): utterance demands the intersection ('both in the same film'); strict AND returns 10/10 genuine both-genre films — assert intersection, not the mix the old max_genre cap encoded.

utterance: [HOLE] I want a musical thriller - a tense suspense film where songs and music are woven into the plot, not just background
note: Musical+Thriller AND-pool is near-empty; needs OR-fallback when candidate count is below k

utterance: My 7-year-old wants a movie tonight—animated, PG or below
note: PG ceiling not enforced; R-rated content surfaces in results

utterance: I only have 80 minutes—show me something funny I can actually finish
note: 80-minute ceiling ignored; long dramas crowd out short comedies

utterance: Suggest a good thriller—early morning tomorrow, so nothing over two hours
note: 120-min cap + thriller-vibe routing. mood='thriller' -> resolve_mood -> suspense/tense is LOAD-BEARING: without it liked_genres=[Thriller] alone surfaces only 1/10 thrillers (co-watch/popularity flood). contains Thriller>=5 (10/10) is the retrieval test; a Drama cap is intentionally omitted (this corpus co-tags thrillers Drama).

utterance: I loved Toy Story but want something fresh—no sequels, nothing from a franchise
note: Toy Story sequels flood top-k when franchise filter is unbuilt

utterance: Family movie night with the teenagers—adventure or comedy, nothing R-rated
note: PG-13 ceiling absent; R-rated action titles enter despite family context

utterance: Want a quick animated watch—under 80 minutes and not part of any series or franchise
note: Compound constraint (max_runtime + exclude_franchise) fails silently; neither slot built

utterance: [HOLE] French comedies under 90 minutes—original French language, nothing dubbed
note: Language constraint has no slot in extraction shape or corpus metadata | step C: require_language=fr + max_runtime compose; Comedy>=5 relies on the thread-2 genre re-rank to out-rank French docs.

utterance: [ORACLE] build_facet_store ground-truth on Toy Story
note: Independent ground-truth check on baked facet values — catches a build_facet_store units/parse/collection regression the self-consistent gates miss.

utterance: Gory slasher horror night, but PG-13 max — nothing R
note: Adversarial: horror pool is R-heavy, so a no-op rating filter surfaces R titles and max_rating bites (exercises the drop path).

utterance: movies like Pulp Fiction (no vibe tags extracted — Mode-1.5 must inject them)
note: pure-title request (empty genome_tags): Mode-1.5 injects Pulp Fiction's own discriminative genome tags (hit men/dark humor/tarantino) so the re-rank surfaces crime neighbours and kills the 1994 era-drift (Forrest Gump/Toy Story)

utterance: movies like Amélie (no vibe tags extracted — Mode-1.5 must inject them)
note: pure-title request (empty genome_tags): Mode-1.5 injects Amélie's own tags (feel-good/whimsical/imagination) so whimsical-romance neighbours surface. DIFFERENTIAL-VERIFIED: with the lever off the seed drifts to 2000-2004 action/thriller co-watch (Kill Bill @6, Memento @5) and these assertions FAIL.

utterance: movies actually set in Paris during World War II — the occupation, not just any war film
note: compound require_genome_tags must AND (each tag clears GENOME_HARD_FLOOR), not average: Schindler's List (wwii 0.99 / paris 0.01, mean 0.50 >= floor) must be DROPPED; only genuine Paris-set WWII films (Army of Shadows, Au revoir les enfants, Casablanca) survive.

utterance: show me black and white films — classic monochrome cinema
note: step C Engine-1a format facet: require_attributes=black and white (TMDB keyword, 364 films).

utterance: I'd like some films directed by women
note: step C Engine-1a attribute facet: 'directed by women' -> woman-director TMDB keyword (560 films).

utterance: give me movies based on a book
note: step C Engine-1a attribute facet: 'based on a book' -> based-on-novel-or-book keyword (1149 films).

utterance: going to Tokyo soon — movies based in Japan, no animation or anime
note: API-wiring LOCATION hard filter: 'based in Japan' -> require_genome_tags japan floor (0.35 AND); exclude_genres drops anime/animation. Setting (japan) is DISTINCT from require_country (JP nationality). japan floor pool = 335 films; top-10 Japan-set, zero Animation.

utterance: I want to watch some Japanese movies

assertions.title: Spirited Away (Sen to Chihiro no kamikakushi) (2001)

note: NATIONALITY vs SETTING (sibling to the 'based in Japan' genome case above): 'japanese movies' -> require_country JP. The gate reads origin_country (nationality), NOT production_countries: co-financed US films (Cliffhanger prod FR/IT/US/JP, Scott Pilgrim prod JP/GB/US — both origin US) must NOT leak in. The excludes_title_substr assertions are RED under the old production_countries source and GREEN under origin_country; Spirited Away (origin JP) stays. all_have_country counts co-pros as matches so it alone cannot catch the leak.

utterance: movies about racing, street racing or track racing
note: A PURE require_genome_tags request must TASTE-RANK within its floor, not collapse to popularity. Before the anchor-seeding fix (llm_frontend.py ~1195), require_genome_tags seeded no anchors -> fallback=True -> the 0.35 racing floor is a binary gate that admits incidental blockbusters (Star Wars racing=0.358, Raiders 0.377) which popularity floats to the top over real racing films (Rush=0.998). After: require_gt seeds the anchors -> fallback=False -> top-12 are genuine racing films (F&F / Need for Speed / Rush / Death Race / Cars, racing>=0.6). excludes Star Wars/Raiders + min_genome racing>=0.5 x8 are RED before, GREEN after.
